title: Context rot: why stuffing the context window hurts accuracy

body.problem: You have a large context window (up to 1M tokens) so you pack in as much information as possible — full documents, verbose tool results, entire conversation histories. But Claude's responses become less accurate and miss relevant details that are clearly in context.
body.solution: Curate what goes into the context window. A larger window allows more, but accuracy and recall degrade as token count grows — a phenomenon called 'context rot'. Use strategies like server-side compaction, tool result clearing, and thinking block clearing to keep context lean and relevant.
body.keyTakeaway: Managing what's IN your context matters as much as how much space is available. Treat context like a curated briefing, not a dumping ground.
